title: Marth Mariyam Major Archiepiscopal Church
tagline: Marth Mariyam Major Archiepiscopal Church — spiritual town of Kerala

heroImage.alt: Marth Mariyam Major Archiepiscopal Church, Kerala

overview.short: The Marth Mariam Syro-Malabar Church is a church belonging to the Syro-Malabar Catholic Church located in Kuravilangad in Kottayam district, India.
overview.description: The Marth Mariam Syro-Malabar Church is a church belonging to the Syro-Malabar Catholic Church located in Kuravilangad in Kottayam district, India. It is considered the Mother Church of the St.Thomas Christians of India. Claiming to date to 105 AD, the church has an ancient bell with the engraving “Mother of God” in the Syriac language; three majestic bells were brought from Germany in 1911 and are among the largest in Asia. The church is also known for its Kappalottam or "racing ship," a commemoration of the biblical story of Jonah and the Whale.

topPlaces.name: Kozha Sree Narasimhaswami Temple
topPlaces.description: Kozhaa Sree Narasimhaswami Temple is a Hindu shrine at Kozha, in the village of Kuravilangad, Kottayam, Kerala, in India. The temple is dedicated to Narasimha, the fourth incarnation of the Hindu god Vishnu.

topPlaces.name: Kuravilangad
topPlaces.description: Kuravilangad is an Indian town located in the northern part of the Kottayam district in Kerala. It is situated in the Meenachil taluk, about 22 km north of the district capital Kottayam and 17 km west of the municipal town Pala.

topPlaces.name: Kanjirathanam
topPlaces.description: Kanjirathanam is a small town in Kottayam District, Kerala, India located between kuruppanthara and Kuravilangad.

topPlaces.name: Kanjikkuzhi
topPlaces.description: Kanjikuzhi is a census town in Kottayam district in the Indian state of Kerala. It is a suburb of Kottayam City.

topPlaces.name: Kurichithanam
topPlaces.description: Kurichithanam is a village in Kottayam district in the state of Kerala, India. K. R. Narayanan, a former president of India, was born in this village.

topPlaces.name: Kappumthala
topPlaces.description: Kappumthala is a village in Kottayam district in the state of Kerala, India. The nearest railway station is at Kuruppanthara and Vaikom road.

topPlaces.name: Vayala
topPlaces.description: Vayala is a village located in the Meenachil (Pala) Taluk of Kottayam district, Kerala, India. It is approximately 5 kilometers from the village Kuravilangad, about 11 kilometers from Pala municipality town and about 24 kilometers away from district capital Kottayam.

topPlaces.name: Pattupurackal Bhagavathy Temple
topPlaces.description: Pattupurackal Bhagavathy Temple (also known as Kattampack Kizhakkum Bhagam Pattupurackal Bhagavathy temple) is an ancient (dating back to the 10th century CE) Bhadrakali temple located in Njeezhoor village, Kottayam, Kerala. It is the only temple in Njeezhoor village where the presiding deity is Bhadrakali.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Marth Mariyam Major Archiepiscopal Church?
faq.a: The best time to visit Marth Mariyam Major Archiepiscopal Church is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Marth Mariyam Major Archiepiscopal Church?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Cochin International Airport, Kochi (48 km) and the nearest railway station is Ernakulam Junction (Kochi) (39 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Marth Mariyam Major Archiepiscopal Church?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Marth Mariyam Major Archiepiscopal Church at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Marth Mariyam Major Archiepiscopal Church?
faq.a: Stays in Marth Mariyam Major Archiepiscopal Church start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Marth Mariyam Major Archiepiscopal Church?
faq.a: The top attractions are Kozha Sree Narasimhaswami Temple, Kuravilangad, Kanjirathanam, Kanjikkuzhi, Kurichithanam.
